text: Abstract This chapter examines the variables affecting the adoption and implementation of artificial intelligence (AI) in Africa. This chapter introduces the concept of the term “Responsible AI” with respect to the African context. This chapter further elaborates the effects of low literacy, lack of sufficient skills in the field of AI in Africa, as well as certain obstacles that can hinder the implementation of AI technologies. This chapter also focuses on the ecosystem of the start-ups and the research institutions in the field of AI within the African region. It highlights the significant projects that are worth mentioning, as well as the challenges faced by the key players in this sector and trends that need to be addressed. “Challenges to effective AI adoption and Implementation in Africa” culminates by explaining the concept of the term “Responsible AI” within the context of Africa and includes several recommendations on how to achieve such a goal.
